response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack developer with over 20 years in web, mobile, and some embedded contexts, but he is fundamentally mismatched with this role's core requirements. The most critical disqualifier is the complete absence of Japanese language proficiency — the role requires native-level Japanese, while Bogdan lists only English, Romanian, and basic German. Beyond language, his primary technical stack is TypeScript and Node.js, with C/C++ appearing only marginally (NI LabWindows components, a thesis project); there is no evidence of Linux kernel-level development, embedded networking security (MACsec, 802.1X), or LAN-switch product experience.
strengths: 20+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, and early embedded systems, demonstrating adaptability
Demonstrated experience with Linux environments and low-latency systems (Sociomantic micro-services, Sound Position Server with C++/Linux/JACK)
REST API and infrastructure knowledge (Docker, CI/CD, GitLab, Kubernetes) is relevant to the networking management plane
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — this is a hard, non-negotiable requirement for the role
No evidence of C-language embedded firmware development, Linux kernel modification, or any security protocol implementation (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal)
No networking domain experience at Layer 2/3, no switch/chip SDK knowledge, and no embedded security background
